records.ety: [L. mysticus, Gr. <?/ belonging to secret rites, from <?/ one initiated: cf. F. mystique. See 1st Mystery, Misty.]

defs: Remote from or beyond human comprehension; baffling human understanding; unknowable; obscure; mysterious.
quotes: Heaven's numerous hierarchy span The mystic gulf from God to man.
God hath revealed a way mystical and supernatural.

defs: Importing or implying mysticism; involving some secret meaning; allegorical; emblematical; as, a mystic dance; mystic Babylon.
quotes: Thus, then, did the spirit of unity and meekness inspire every joint and sinew of the mystical body.

defs: employing mysticism; as, mystical intuition; mystical explanations; -- contrasted to logical, rational, analytical.
